item.title: Paper Division
item.description: Introduction In origami, there may be times where a 13 x 13 grid or an 11 x 11 grid is needed. Such “indivisble” grids are hard to fold without measuring or cutting strips off, since realistically, the paper can only be accurately bisected. Method One way to fold an N x N grid is to use reference points. Let us plot a square on a Cartesian plane, with the bottom-left corner of the square placed about the origin.

item.title: Grade 11 Chemistry
item.description: Google Doc Notes Link Grade 10 Review Zac’s Gr 11 Chem Notes Zac’s Gr 10 Chem Notes Gr 9 Science Notes Gr 10 Chem Notes Mixtures & Pure Substances Pure substances cannot be broken down by physical means - only chemical Substances contain 2+ substances not joined together by chemical means Mixtures Homogenous (aka solution) Uniform appearance Heterogeneous Distinctly different substances can be seen (phases) Pure substances Element Atoms Molecules 2+ atoms bonded together Compound Molecules 2+ different atoms bonded together Nomenclature Ionic compounds: Two elements: metal name + non-metal root + “ide” E.
